acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-core` passes (core builds in isolation)
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-rs` passes
- [ ] beads-core has ZERO dependencies on beads-rs modules
- [ ] `beads_rs::core::BeadId` still resolves
- [ ] All tests pass
description:
**Problem**
`crates/beads-rs/src/core/**` needs to become standalone `beads-core` crate. Currently core has dependencies on:
- `crate::error::{Effect, Transience}` (must move into core)
- Tests in `core/state.rs` call `crate::git::wire::serialize_*` (must move tests out)

Note: enum_str! macro extraction is handled separately in bd-14fs.11.

**Changes**

1. **Move Effect + Transience into beads-core**:
   - Define `beads_core::Effect` and `beads_core::Transience` (in `core/effect.rs` or `core/error.rs`)
   - Update `core/error.rs` to use local types instead of `crate::error::{Effect, Transience}`
   - In beads-rs: `pub use beads_core::{Effect, Transience};`
   - Update `crates/beads-rs/src/error.rs` to use `beads_core::{Effect, Transience}`

2. **Move directory**: `crates/beads-rs/src/core/**` → `crates/beads-core/src/**`
   - `crates/beads-core/src/lib.rs` mirrors old `core/mod.rs` structure
   - Keep `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`

3. **Update beads-rs to re-export**:
   - Replace `pub mod core;` with `pub use beads_core as core;`
   - This preserves `beads_rs::core::BeadId` etc.

4. **Move git-dependent tests out of core**:
   - `core/state.rs` tests that call `crate::git::wire::serialize_*` → move to `crates/beads-rs/tests/core_state_fingerprint.rs`
   - Rule: **no beads-core tests depend on daemon/git/IPC modules**

5. **Update Cargo.toml files**:
   - `beads-core/Cargo.toml`: add `beads-macros` dependency, copy relevant deps from beads-rs
   - `beads-rs/Cargo.toml`: add `beads-core` dependency

**Files**
- crates/beads-core/src/** (moved from beads-rs/src/core/**)
- crates/beads-rs/src/lib.rs (re-export as core)
- crates/beads-rs/src/error.rs (use beads_core types)
- crates/beads-rs/tests/core_state_fingerprint.rs (new - moved tests)
- crates/beads-core/Cargo.toml
- crates/beads-rs/Cargo.toml
title: Phase 1: Extract core into beads-core

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] beads-git crate exists and compiles independently
- [ ] beads-daemon-core owns WAL impl with feature-gated sqlite
- [ ] beads-daemon owns daemon runtime (server, executor, mutation engine, query, store, admin, repl orchestration)
- [ ] beads-rs is a thin facade (<6k lines source)
- [ ] Zero beads_rs::daemon::* imports outside beads-rs itself
- [ ] DAG policy covers all crates including dev-deps
- [ ] cargo check/test/clippy/dylint all pass
description:
**Problem**
beads-rs has a 38k-line daemon monolith and a 10k-line git module inside a single crate. Despite good internal boundaries (Phase II), crate-level enforcement is incomplete — agents can still couple across subsystem boundaries because \`pub mod daemon\` exposes everything. The codebase needs compiler-enforced isolation (crate boundaries as locked rooms) for local reasoning, agent-proofing, and eventual cross-language consumption.

**Design**
Decompose beads-rs into well-bounded crates. One new crate (\`beads-git\`), expand two existing ones (\`beads-daemon-core\` absorbs WAL impl, \`beads-daemon\` absorbs daemon runtime). beads-rs shrinks to a ~5k-line thin facade.

End state:
- beads-core (20k) — types, CRDTs, events (unchanged)
- beads-api (2k) — IPC schemas (unchanged)
- beads-surface (4k) — IPC client, ops/query (unchanged)
- beads-cli (9k) — CLI parse/dispatch (unchanged)
- beads-git (10k) — NEW: git sync + checkpoint
- beads-daemon-core (14k) — EXPAND: WAL impl + repl protocol + contracts
- beads-daemon (23k) — EXPAND: full daemon runtime
- beads-rs (5k) — SHRINK: binary entrypoint + config + compat

Critical path:
- Policy cleanup (unblocks enforcement)
- Injection types + break reverse dep (unblocks extraction, parallelizable)
- Extract beads-git (parallelizable with WAL move)
- Absorb WAL into daemon-core (parallelizable with git extraction)
- Move daemon runtime into beads-daemon (blocked by above)
- Add testkit + migrate fixtures (blocked by daemon move)
- Narrow beads-rs exports (final cleanup)

**Acceptance**
- [ ] \`beads-git\` crate exists with git sync + checkpoint, zero daemon imports
- [ ] \`beads-daemon-core\` owns full WAL implementation (feature-gated)
- [ ] \`beads-daemon\` owns full daemon runtime with \`pub(crate)\` internals
- [ ] \`beads-rs\` is <6k lines, no \`pub mod daemon\`
- [ ] All tests pass, all boundary lints pass
- [ ] DAG policy updated and enforced including dev-deps
- [ ] An agent dropped into any single crate can reason about it locally

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Administrative closure audit on 2026-03-13 from /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs.

Epic re-audited during `beads-rs-gou3.8` closeout. All 9 children are closed, the remaining public-boundary leaks were finished in follow-on epic `beads-rs-gou3`, and the current verification matrix for that hard-cutover work is green. Leaving this epic open no longer reflects repo or tracker reality.

title: Phase III: crate decomposition for local reasoning + agent-proofing

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `RepoState.state` is `StoreState`, not `CanonicalState`.
- [ ] `apply_event` is only called on the `CanonicalState` for the event’s namespace.
- [ ] Checkpoint snapshot/export supports non-core namespaces (no hard error for non-core).
- [ ] Queries and IPC handlers require namespace explicitly or use a single defaulting rule.
- [ ] Tests cover same BeadId in different namespaces without collision.
description:
**Problem**
EventBody carries `namespace`, watermarks are namespace-scoped, but daemon state is still a single `CanonicalState`. `apply_event` ignores namespace and `CheckpointSnapshot` rejects any namespace other than core. This makes namespaces a runtime convention instead of a type-level boundary, allows ID collisions across namespaces, and spreads conditional logic across the codebase.

**Signals / Evidence**
- `RepoState.state` is `CanonicalState` (no namespace) (`src/daemon/repo.rs`).
- `apply_event(&mut CanonicalState, &EventBody)` ignores `EventBody.namespace` (`src/core/apply.rs`).
- `checkpoint` snapshot builder rejects non-core namespaces (`src/git/checkpoint/export.rs`).
- Namespace-aware types exist (`StoreState`, `NamespaceId`) but are not wired through daemon state.

**Why this hurts velocity**
We keep adding namespace-aware code (watermarks, policies, repl), but the core state remains global. Every new feature needs bespoke checks, and any future multi-namespace work becomes a large invasive refactor.
design:
**Design**
1. Promote `RepoState.state` to `StoreState` and make namespace the primary boundary.
2. Add a thin helper `apply_event_to_namespace(store_state, body)` that routes to `StoreState::ensure_namespace(body.namespace)` and calls existing `apply_event` on the selected `CanonicalState`.
3. Update mutation + repl ingest paths to use the namespace-scoped state.
4. Update checkpoint snapshot/builders to accept `StoreState` and remove the core-only restriction.
5. Audit query paths to require/propagate namespace (most are already in `NormalizedReadConsistency`).
6. Add cross-namespace isolation tests (same BeadId in different namespaces does not conflict).

**Design Notes**
Keep `CanonicalState` unchanged; `StoreState` should be the only place that holds per-namespace maps. This keeps CRDT algebra stable while encoding the boundary in types.
title: Namespace boundary not encoded in state (StoreState migration)

acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] It is impossible to represent a sealed segment without `final_len` (compile‑time enforced).
- [ ] DB load rejects invalid rows before creating `SegmentRow`.
- [ ] Replay/fsck no longer needs to check for sealed+None at runtime.
- [ ] Tests cover open vs sealed row serialization and invalid DB row rejection.
description:
**Problem**
`SegmentRow` in the WAL index uses `sealed: bool` + `final_len: Option<u64>`. This allows invalid states like `sealed=true` with `final_len=None`, which are only caught at replay/fsck time.

Key refs:
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/index.rs:198` (SegmentRow)
- `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/wal/replay.rs:264` (runtime error when sealed missing final_len)

**Impact**
Index invariants are not enforced at compile time; sealed segment metadata can be corrupt without immediate detection.
design:
**Design (opinionated)**
Make sealed‑segment invariants structural.

Option A (enum row):
```rust
enum SegmentRow {
  Open { /* fields */, last_indexed_offset: u64 },
  Sealed { /* fields */, final_len: u64 },
}
```
- No `Option` for final_len; sealed implies `final_len` exists.
- Encode/decode to DB enforces the invariant when loading rows.

Option B (struct + NonZero marker):
- `final_len: FinalLen` where `FinalLen` is only constructible when sealed, but enum is cleaner.

Update replay/index logic to accept typed rows so invalid state cannot compile.
title: Encode WAL segment sealed/final_len invariants

description:
Problem: admin-metrics snapshot is cumulative across daemon lifetime, so request histogram maxima include startup/warm-up operations and can mislead steady-state comparisons (e.g., ready max).

Design: add a daemon metrics reset/snapshot-since capability (or benchmark-side daemon restart before measured phase) so steady-state histograms can be isolated without manual parsing.

Acceptance:
- [ ] Benchmark can report steady-state admin histograms without startup contamination
- [ ] docs/PERF_HOTPATHS.md documents expected behavior and workflow
- [ ] regression test or scripted check validates boundary behavior
title: Admin metrics include startup warm-up outliers in hotpath runs
